narrative:
The Zebras are positioned for a high-ceiling offensive outing today, headlined by Pete Crow-Armstrong and Yordan Alvarez, who anchor the lineup with projected scores exceeding 100 points. Crow-Armstrong benefits significantly from a favorable platoon advantage against Jake Irvin and a high-efficiency catcher matchup, while Alvarez maintains elite status with a +30% SP skill adjustment against Jack Kochanowicz. Trea Turner remains a high-floor play in the leadoff spot; his 15% BvP edge against MacKenzie Gore offsets the moderate SP skill of the matchup, making him a prime candidate for stolen base production despite the elite hold-on tendency of the opposing battery.

Conversely, the bottom of the order requires monitoring as Logan O'Hoppe and Carson Benge face negative pressure from projected #8-spot penalties and suboptimal platoon splits. Kyle Schwarber faces a stern test against the lefty-lefty matchup with Gore, which suppresses his projected output despite his elite xwOBA metrics. With several hitters—notably O'Hoppe, Benge, and Yelich—navigating restrictive platoon or lineup-order headwinds, our 5x5 Roto efficiency will rely heavily on the top-half production to carry the day. Ensure all lineup confirmations are locked prior to first pitch to mitigate the risk of late-scratches impacting these projected order-based premiums.
